These should be fetched from book_providers, but can't cause circular\n   212→            # dep\n   213→            'id_project_gutenberg',\n   214→            'id_project_runeberg',\n   215→            'id_librivox',\n   216→            'id_standard_ebooks',\n   217→            'id_openstax',\n   218→            'id_cita_press',\n   219→            'id_wikisource',\n   220→        }\n   221→    )\n   222→    facet_rewrites = MappingProxyType(\n   223→        {\n   224→            ('public_scan', 'true'): 'ebook_access:public',\n   225→            ('public_scan', 'false'): '-ebook_access:public',\n   226→            ('print_disabled', 'true'): 'ebook_access:printdisabled',\n   227→            ('print_disabled', 'false'): '-ebook_access:printdisabled',\n   228→            (\n   229→                'has_fulltext',\n   230→                'true',\n   231→            ): lambda: f'ebook_access:[{get_fulltext_min()} TO *]',\n   232→            (\n   233→                'has_fulltext',\n   234→                'false',\n   235→            ): lambda: f'ebook_access:[* TO {get_fulltext_min()}]',\n   236→        }\n   237→    )\n   238→    # These are extra public api params on top of facets, which are also public\n   239→    public_api_params = frozenset(\n   240→        {\n   241→            'title',\n   242→            'publisher',\n   243→            'oclc',\n   244→            'lccn',\n   245→            'contributor',\n   246→            'subject',\n   247→            'place',\n   248→            'person',\n   249→            'time',\n   250→            'author_key',\n   251→            'author',\n   252→            'isbn',\n   253→        }\n   254→    )\n   255→\n   256→    def is_search_field(self, field: str):\n   257→        # New variable introduced to prevent rewriting the input.\n   258→        if field.startswith(('work.', 'edition.')):\n   259→            return self.is_search_field(field.partition(\".\")[2])\n   260→        return super().is_search_field(field) or field.startswith('id_')\n   261→\n   262→    def transform_user_query(\n   263→        self, user_query: str, q_tree: luqum.tree.Item\n   264→    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n   265→        has_search_fields = False\n   266→        for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n   267→            if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField):\n   268→                has_search_fields = True\n   269→                if node.name.lower() in self.field_name_map:\n   270→                    node.name = self.field_name_map[node.name.lower()]\n   271→                if node.name == 'isbn':\n   272→                    isbn_transform(node)\n   273→                if node.name in ('lcc', 'lcc_sort'):\n   274→                    lcc_transform(node)\n   275→                if node.name in ('dcc', 'dcc_sort'):\n   276→                    ddc_transform(node)\n   277→\n   278→        if not has_search_fields:\n   279→            # If there are no search fields, maybe we want just an isbn?\n   280→            isbn = normalize_isbn(user_query)\n   281→            if isbn and len(isbn) in (10, 13):\n   282→                q_tree = luqum_parser(f'isbn:({isbn})')\n   283→\n   284→        return q_tree\n   285→\n   286→    def build_q_from_params(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n   287→        q_list = []\n   288→        for k in self.public_api_params & set(params):\n   289→            values = params[k] if isinstance(params[k], list) else [params[k]]\n   290→            for val in values:\n   291→                if k == 'author':\n   292→                    v = val.strip()\n   293→                    m = re_author_key.search(v)\n   294→                    if m:\n   295→                        q_list.append(f\"author_key:({m.group(1)})\")\n   296→                    else:\n   297→                        v = fully_escape_query(v)\n   298→                        q_list.append(\n   299→                            f\"(author_name:({v}) OR author_alternative_name:({v}))\"\n   300→                        )\n   301→                elif k == 'isbn':\n   302→                    normalized = normalize_isbn(val)\n   303→                    q_list.append(f'isbn:({normalized or val})')\n   304→                else:\n   305→                    q_list.append(f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(val)})')\n   306→\n   307→        return ' AND '.join(q_list)\n   308→\n   309→    def q_to_solr_params(  # noqa: PLR0915, C901\n   310→        self,\n   311→        q: str,\n   312→        solr_fields: set[str],\n   313→        cur_solr_params: list[tuple[str, str]],\n   314→        highlight: bool = False,\n   315→    ) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n   316→        new_params: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\n   317→\n   318→        # We need to parse the tree so that it gets transformed using the\n   319→        # special OL query parsing rules (different from default solr!)\n   320→        # See luqum_parser for details.\n   321→        work_q_tree = luqum_parser(q)\n   322→\n   323→        # Removes the work prefix from fields; used as the callable argument for 'luqum_replace_field'\n   324→        def remove_work_prefix(field: str) -> str:\n   325→            return field.partition('.')[2] if field.startswith('work.') else field\n   326→\n   327→        # Removes the indicator prefix from queries with the 'work field' before appending them to parameters.\n   328→        final_work_query = deepcopy(work_q_tree)\n   329→        luqum_replace_field(final_work_query, remove_work_prefix)\n   330→        try:\n   331→            luqum_remove_field(final_work_query, lambda f: f.startswith('edition.'))\n   332→        except EmptyTreeError:\n   333→            # If the whole tree is removed, we should just search for everything\n   334→            final_work_query = luqum_parser('*:*')\n   335→\n   336→        new_params.append(('userWorkQuery', str(final_work_query)))\n   337→\n   338→        # This full work query uses solr-specific syntax to add extra parameters\n   339→        # to the way the search is processed. We are using the edismax parser.\n   340→        # See https://solr.apache.org/guide/8_11/the-extended-dismax-query-parser.html\n   341→        # This is somewhat synonymous to setting defType=edismax in the\n   342→        # query, but much more flexible. We wouldn't be able to do our\n   343→        # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\n   344→\n   345→        full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\"AND\" qf=\"{qf}\" pf=\"{pf}\" bf=\"{bf}\" v={v}}})'.format(\n   346→            # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\n   347→            # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\n   348→            # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\n   349→            qf='text alternative_title^10 author_name^10 chapter^5',\n   350→            # pf: phrase fields. This increases the score of documents that\n   351→            # match the query terms in close proximity to each other.\n   352→            pf='alternative_title^10 author_name^10',\n   353→            # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\n   354→            # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\n   355→            # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\n   356→            # quality.\n   357→            bf='min(100,edition_count) min(100,def(readinglog_count,0))',\n   358→            # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser. Note\n   359→            # we are using a solr variable here; this reads the url parameter\n   360→            # arbitrarily called userWorkQuery.\n   361→            v='$userWorkQuery',\n   362→        )\n   363→        ed_q = None\n   364→        full_ed_query = None\n   365→        editions_fq = []\n   366→        if req_context.get().solr_editions and 'editions:[subquery]' in solr_fields:\n   367→            WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD: dict[str, str | Callable[[str], str]] = {\n   368→                # Internals\n   369→                'edition_key': 'key',\n   370→                'text': 'text',\n   371→                # Display data\n   372→                'title': 'title',\n   373→                'title_suggest': 'title_suggest',\n   374→                'subtitle': 'subtitle',\n   375→                'alternative_title': 'alternative_title',\n   376→                'alternative_subtitle': 'subtitle',\n   377→                'cover_i': 'cover_i',\n   378→                # Duplicate author fields\n   379→                # Disabled until the next full reindex\n   380→                # 'author_name': 'author_name',\n   381→                # 'author_key': 'author_key',\n   382→                # 'author_alternative_name': 'author_alternative_name',\n   383→                # 'author_facet': 'author_facet',\n   384→                # Misc useful data\n   385→                'format': 'format',\n   386→                'language': 'language',\n   387→                'chapter': 'chapter',\n   388→                'publisher': 'publisher',\n   389→                'publisher_facet': 'publisher_facet',\n   390→                'publish_date': 'publish_date',\n   391→                'publish_year': 'publish_year',\n   392→                # Identifiers\n   393→                'isbn': 'isbn',\n   394→                # 'id_*': 'id_*', # Handled manually for now to match any id field\n   395→                'ebook_access': 'ebook_access',\n   396→                # IA\n   397→                'has_fulltext': 'has_fulltext',\n   398→                'ia': 'ia',\n   399→                'ia_collection': 'ia_collection',\n   400→                'ia_box_id': 'ia_box_id',\n   401→                'public_scan_b': 'public_scan_b',\n   402→            }\n   403→\n   404→            def convert_work_field_to_edition_field(\n   405→                field: str,\n   406→            ) -> str | Callable[[str], str] | None:\n   407→                \"\"\"\n   408→                Convert a SearchField name (eg 'title') to the correct fieldname\n   409→                for use in an edition query.\n   410→\n   411→                If no conversion is possible, return None.\n   412→                \"\"\"\n   413→                if field in WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD:\n   414→                    return WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD[field]\n   415→                elif field.startswith('id_'):\n   416→                    return field\n   417→                elif self.is_search_field(field) or field in self.facet_fields:\n   418→                    return None\n   419→                else:\n   420→                    raise ValueError(f'Unknown field: {field}')\n   421→\n   422→            def convert_work_query_to_edition_query(work_query: str) -> str:\n   423→                \"\"\"\n   424→                Convert a work query to an edition query. Mainly involves removing\n   425→                invalid fields, or renaming fields as necessary.\n   426→                \"\"\"\n   427→                q_tree = luqum_parser(work_query)\n   428→                for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n   429→                    if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField) and node.name != '*':\n   430→                        if node.name.startswith('edition.'):\n   431→                            ed_field = node.name.partition('.')[2]\n   432→                        else:\n   433→                            ed_field = node.name\n   434→\n   435→                        new_name = convert_work_field_to_edition_field(ed_field)\n   436→                        if new_name is None:\n   437→                            try:\n   438→                                luqum_remove_child(node, parents)\n   439→                            except EmptyTreeError:\n   440→                                # Deleted the whole tree! Nothing left\n   441→                                return ''\n   442→                        elif isinstance(new_name, str):\n   443→                            parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n   444→                            # Prefixing with + makes the field mandatory\n   445→                            if isinstance(\n   446→                                parent,\n   447→                                (\n   448→                                    luqum.tree.Not,\n   449→                                    luqum.tree.Prohibit,\n   450→                                    luqum.tree.OrOperation,\n   451→                                ),\n   452→                            ):\n   453→                                node.name = new_name\n   454→                            else:\n   455→                                node.name = f'+{new_name}'\n   456→                            if new_name == 'key':\n   457→                                # need to convert eg 'edition_key:OL123M' to\n   458→                                # 'key:(/books/OL123M)'. Or\n   459→                                # key:(/books/OL123M OR /books/OL456M)\n   460→                                for n, n_parents in luqum_traverse(node.expr):\n   461→                                    if isinstance(\n   462→                                        n, (luqum.tree.Word, luqum.tree.Phrase)\n   463→                                    ):\n   464→                                        val = (\n   465→                                            n.value\n   466→                                            if isinstance(n, luqum.tree.Word)\n   467→                                            else n.value[1:-1]\n   468→                                        )\n   469→                                        val = val.removeprefix('/books/')\n   470→                                        n.value = f'\"/books/{val}\"'\n   471→                        elif callable(new_name):\n   472→                            # Replace this node with a new one\n   473→                            # First process the expr\n   474→                            new_expr = convert_work_query_to_edition_query(\n   475→                                str(node.expr)\n   476→                            )\n   477→                            new_node = luqum.tree.Group(\n   478→                                luqum_parser(new_name(new_expr))\n   479→                            )\n   480→                            if parents:\n   481→                                luqum_replace_child(parents[-1], node, new_node)\n   482→                            else:\n   483→                                return convert_work_query_to_edition_query(\n   484→                                    str(new_node)\n   485→                                )\n   486→                        else:\n   487→                            # Shouldn't happen\n   488→                            raise ValueError(f'Invalid new_name: {new_name}')\n   489→                return str(q_tree)\n   490→\n   491→            # Move over all fq parameters that can be applied to editions.\n   492→            # These are generally used to handle facets.\n   493→            editions_fq = ['type:edition']\n   494→            for param_name, param_value in cur_solr_params:\n   495→                if param_name != 'fq' or param_value.startswith('type:'):\n   496→                    continue\n   497→                field_name, field_val = param_value.split(':', 1)\n   498→                if ed_field := convert_work_field_to_edition_field(field_name):\n   499→                    editions_fq.append(f'{ed_field}:{field_val}')\n   500→            for fq in editions_fq:\n   501→                new_params.append(('editions.fq', fq))\n   502→\n   503→            user_lang = convert_iso_to_marc(self.lang) or 'eng'\n   504→\n   505→            ed_q = convert_work_query_to_edition_query(str(work_q_tree))\n   506→            # Note that if there is no edition query (because no fields in\n   507→            # the user's work query apply), we use the special value *:* to\n   508→            # match everything, but still get boosting.\n   509→            new_params.append(('userEdQuery', ed_q or '*:*'))\n   510→            # Needs to also set this on the editions subquery; subqueries appear\n   511→            # to have their own scope for template parameters, so in order\n   512→            # for `userEdQuery` to be available to `editions.q`, we will\n   513→            # need to specify it twice.\n   514→            new_params.append(('editions.userEdQuery', ed_q or '*:*'))\n   515→            new_params.append(('editions.ol.label', 'EDITION_MATCH'))\n   516→\n   517→            full_ed_query = '({{!edismax bq=\"{bq}\" v={v} qf=\"{qf}\"}})'.format(\n   518→                # See qf in work_query\n   519→                qf='text alternative_title^4 author_name^4 chapter^4',\n   520→                # Reading from the url parameter userEdQuery. This lets us avoid\n   521→                # having to try to escape the query in order to fit inside this\n   522→                # other query.\n   523→                v='$userEdQuery',\n   524→                # bq (boost query): Boost which edition is promoted to the top\n   525→                bq=' '.join(\n   526→                    (\n   527→                        f'language:{user_lang}^40',\n   528→                        'ebook_access:public^10',\n   529→                        'ebook_access:borrowable^8',\n   530→                        'ebook_access:printdisabled^2',\n   531→                        'cover_i:*^2',\n   532→                    )\n   533→                ),\n   534→            )\n   535→\n   536→        if ed_q or len(editions_fq) > 1:\n   537→            # The elements in _this_ edition query should cause works not to\n   538→            # match _at all_ if matching editions are not found\n   539→            new_params.append(\n   540→                ('fullEdQuery', cast(str, full_ed_query) if ed_q else '*:*')\n   541→            )\n   542→            q = (\n   543→                f'+{full_work_query} '\n   544→                # This is using the special parent query syntax to, on top of\n   545→                # the user's `full_work_query`, also only find works which have\n   546→                # editions matching the edition query.\n   547→                # Also include edition-less works (i.e. edition_count:0)\n   548→                '+('\n   549→                '_query_:\"{!parent which=type:work v=$fullEdQuery filters=$editions.fq}\" '\n   550→                'OR edition_count:0'\n   551→                ')'\n   552→            )\n   553→            new_params.append(('q', q))\n   554→        else:\n   555→            new_params.append(('q', full_work_query))\n   556→\n   557→        if highlight:\n   558→            highlight_fields = ('subject', 'chapter')\n   559→            try:\n   560→                # This can throw the EmptyTreeError if nothing remains in the query\n   561→                highlight_query = luqum_deepcopy(work_q_tree)\n   562→                luqum_remove_field(\n   563→                    highlight_query,\n   564→                    lambda f: f not in highlight_fields,\n   565→                )\n   566→                new_params.append(('hl', 'true'))\n   567→                # NOTE: This only applies to the work, really\n   568→                new_params.append(('hl.fl', ','.join(highlight_fields)))\n   569→                new_params.append(('hl.q', str(highlight_query)))\n   570→                new_params.append(('hl.snippets', '10'))\n   571→                # we can't trim e.g. chapter since it has a specific structure with the pipes\n   572→                new_params.append(('hl.fragsize', '0'))\n   573→            except EmptyTreeError:\n   574→                # nothing to highlight\n   575→                pass\n   576→\n   577→        if full_ed_query:\n   578→            edition_fields = {\n   579→                f.split('.', 1)[1] for f in solr_fields if f.startswith('editions.')\n   580→            }\n   581→            if not edition_fields:\n   582→                edition_fields = solr_fields - {\n   583→                    # Default to same fields as for the work...\n   584→                    'editions:[subquery]',\n   585→                    # but exclude the author fields since they're primarily work data;\n   586→                    # they only exist on editions to improve search matches.\n   587→                    'author_name',\n   588→                    'author_key',\n   589→                    'author_alternative_name',\n   590→                    'author_facet',\n   591→                }\n   592→            # The elements in _this_ edition query will match but not affect\n   593→            # whether the work appears in search results\n   594→            new_params.append(\n   595→                (\n   596→                    'editions.q',\n   597→                    # Here we use the special terms parser to only filter the\n   598→                    # editions for a given, already matching work '_root_' node.\n   599→                    f'({{!terms f=_root_ v=$row.key}}) AND {full_ed_query}',\n   600→                )\n   601→            )\n   602→            new_params.append(('editions.rows', '1'))\n   603→            new_params.append(('editions.fl', ','.join(edition_fields)))\n   604→        return new_params\n   605→\n   606→    def add_non_solr_fields(\n   607→        self,\n   608→        non_solr_fields: set[str],\n   609→        solr_result: dict,\n   610→    ) -> None:\n   611→        from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.models import Edition, Work\n   612→\n   613→        prefixed_fields = {\n   614→            prefixed_field\n   615→            for field in non_solr_fields\n   616→            for prefixed_field in (\n   617→                (field,) if '.' in field else (f'work.{field}', f'editions.{field}')\n   618→            )\n   619→        }\n   620→        need_works = any(field.startswith('work.') for field in prefixed_fields)\n   621→        need_editions = any(field.startswith('editions.') for field in prefixed_fields)\n   622→\n   623→        # Augment with data from db\n   624→        keys: list[str] = []\n   625→        if need_works:\n   626→            keys += [doc['key'] for doc in solr_result['response']['docs']]\n   627→\n   628→        if need_editions:\n   629→            keys += [\n   630→                ed_doc['key']\n   631→                for doc in solr_result['response']['docs']\n   632→                for ed_doc in doc.get('editions', {}).get('docs', [])\n   633→            ]\n   634→\n   635→        things = cast(list[Work | Edition], web.ctx.site.get_many(keys))\n   636→        key_to_thing = {t.key: t for t in things if t.key in keys}\n   637→\n   638→        from openlibrary.book_providers import get_acquisitions\n   639→\n   640→        for doc in solr_result['response']['docs']:\n   641→            for field in prefixed_fields:\n   642→                prefix, field_name = field.split('.', 1)\n   643→                if prefix == 'work':\n   644→                    pairs = [(doc, key_to_thing.get(doc['key']))]\n   645→                else:\n   646→                    pairs = [\n   647→                        (ed_doc, key_to_thing.get(ed_doc['key']))\n   648→                        for ed_doc in doc.get('editions', {}).get('docs', [])\n   649→                    ]\n   650→\n   651→                for solr_doc, db_thing in pairs:\n   652→                    # Could be `None` if the record has been deleted and Solr not yet updated.\n   653→                    if not db_thing:\n   654→                        continue\n   655→\n   656→                    val = getattr(db_thing, field_name)\n   657→                    if field_name == 'providers':\n   658→                        ed = cast(Edition, db_thing)\n   659→                        solr_doc[field_name] = [\n   660→                            acq.__dict__ for acq in get_acquisitions(solr_doc, ed)\n   661→                        ]\n   662→                    elif isinstance(val, infogami.infobase.client.Nothing):\n   663→                        continue\n   664→                    elif field_name == 'description':\n   665→                        solr_doc[field_name] = (\n   666→                            val if isinstance(val, str) else val.value\n   667→                        )\n   668→\n   669→\n   670→def lcc_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):\n   671→    # e.g. lcc:[NC1 TO NC1000] to lcc:[NC-0001.00000000 TO NC-1000.00000000]\n   672→    # for proper range search\n   673→    val = sf.children[0]\n   674→    if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):\n   675→        normed_range = normalize_lcc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)\n   676→        if normed_range:\n   677→            val.low.value, val.high.value = normed_range\n   678→    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word):\n   679→        if '*' in val.value and not val.value.startswith('*'):\n   680→            # Marshals human repr into solr repr\n   681→            # lcc:A720* should become A--0720*\n   682→            parts = val.value.split('*', 1)\n   683→            lcc_prefix = normalize_lcc_prefix(parts[0])\n   684→            val.value = (lcc_prefix or parts[0]) + '*' + parts[1]\n   685→        else:\n   686→            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('\"'))\n   687→            if normed:\n   688→                val.value = normed\n   689→    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):\n   690→        normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('\"'))\n   691→        if normed:\n   692→            val.value = f'\"{normed}\"'\n   693→    elif (\n   694→        isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Group)\n   695→        and isinstance(val.expr, luqum.tree.UnknownOperation)\n   696→        and all(isinstance(c, luqum.tree.Word) for c in val.expr.children)\n   697→    ):\n   698→        # treat it as a string\n   699→        normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(str(val.expr))\n   700→        if normed:\n   701→            if ' ' in normed:\n   702→                sf.expr = luqum.tree.Phrase(f'\"{normed}\"')\n   703→            else:\n   704→                sf.expr = luqum.tree.Word(f'{normed}*')\n   705→    else:\n   706→        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected lcc SearchField value type: {type(val)}\")\n   707→\n   708→\n   709→def ddc_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):\n   710→    val = sf.children[0]\n   711→    if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):\n   712→        normed_range = normalize_ddc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)\n   713→        val.low.value = normed_range[0] or val.low\n   714→        val.high.value = normed_range[1] or val.high\n   715→    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) and val.value.endswith('*'):\n   716→        return normalize_ddc_prefix(val.value[:-1]) + '*'\n   717→    elif isinstance(val, (luqum.tree.Word, luqum.tree.Phrase)):\n   718→        if normed := normalize_ddc(val.value.strip('\"')):\n   719→            val.value = normed\n   720→    else:\n   721→        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected ddc transform_user_query(\n        self, user_query: str, q_tree: luqum.tree.Item\n    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n        has_search_fields = False\n        for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n            if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField):\n                has_search_fields = True\n                if node.name.lower() in self.field_name_map:\n                    node.name = self.field_name_map[node.name.lower()]\n                if node.name == 'isbn':\n                    isbn_transform(node)\n                if node.name in ('lcc', 'lcc_sort'):\n                    lcc_transform(node)\n                if node.name in ('dcc', 'dcc_sort'):\n                    ddc_transform(node)\n\n        if not has_search_fields:\n            # If there are no search fields, isinstance(params[k], list) else [params[k]]\n            for val in values:\n                if k == 'author':\n                    v = val.strip()\n                    m = re_author_key.search(v)\n                    if m:\n                        q_list.append(f\"author_key:({m.group(1)})\")\n                    else:\n                        v = fully_escape_query(v)\n                        q_list.append(\n                            f\"(author_name:({v}) OR author_alternative_name:({v}))\"\n                        )\n                elif k == 'isbn':\n                    normalized = highlight: bool = False,\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        new_params: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\n\n        # We need to parse the tree so that it gets transformed using the\n        # special OL query parsing rules (different from default solr!)\n        # See luqum_parser for details.\n        work_q_tree = luqum_parser(q)\n\n        # Removes the work prefix from fields; used as the callable argument for 'luqum_replace_field'\n        def remove_work_prefix(field: str) -> str:\n            return field.partition('.')[2] if field.startswith('work.') else field\n\n        # Removes the indicator prefix from queries with the 'work field' before appending them to parameters.\n        final_work_query = deepcopy(work_q_tree)\n        luqum_replace_field(final_work_query, remove_work_prefix)\n        try:\n            luqum_remove_field(final_work_query, lambda f: f.startswith('edition.'))\n        except EmptyTreeError:\n            # If the whole tree is removed, we should just search for everything\n            final_work_query = luqum_parser('*:*')\n\n        new_params.append(('userWorkQuery', str(final_work_query)))\n\n        # This full work query uses solr-specific syntax to add extra parameters\n        # to the way the search is processed. We are using the edismax parser.\n        # See https://solr.apache.org/guide/8_11/the-extended-dismax-query-parser.html\n        # This is somewhat synonymous to setting defType=edismax in the\n        # query, but much more flexible. We wouldn't be able to do our\n        # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\n\n        full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\"AND\" qf=\"{qf}\" pf=\"{pf}\" bf=\"{bf}\" v={v}}})'.format(\n            # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\n            # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\n            # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\n            qf='text alternative_title^10 author_name^10 chapter^5',\n            # pf: phrase fields. This increases the score of documents that\n            # match the query terms in close proximity to each other.\n            pf='alternative_title^10 author_name^10',\n            # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\n            # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\n            # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\n            # quality.\n            bf='min(100,edition_count) min(100,def(readinglog_count,0))',\n            # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser. Note\n            # we are using a solr variable here; this reads the url parameter\n            # arbitrarily called userWorkQuery.\n            v='$userWorkQuery',\n        )\n        ed_q = None\n        full_ed_query = None\n        editions_fq = []\n        if req_context.get().solr_editions and 'editions:[subquery]' in solr_fields:\n            WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD: dict[str, str | Callable[[str], str]] = {\n                # Internals\n                'edition_key': 'key',\n                'text': 'text',\n                # Display data\n                'title': 'title',\n                'title_suggest': 'title_suggest',\n                'subtitle': 'subtitle',\n                'alternative_title': 'alternative_title',\n                'alternative_subtitle': 'subtitle',\n                'cover_i': 'cover_i',\n                # Duplicate author fields\n                # Disabled until the next full reindex\n                # 'author_name': 'author_name',\n                # 'author_key': 'author_key',\n                # 'author_alternative_name': 'author_alternative_name',\n                # 'author_facet': 'author_facet',\n                # Misc useful data\n                'format': 'format',\n                'language': 'language',\n                'chapter': 'chapter',\n                'publisher': 'publisher',\n                'publisher_facet': 'publisher_facet',\n                'publish_date': 'publish_date',\n                'publish_year': 'publish_year',\n                # Identifiers\n                'isbn': 'isbn',\n                # 'id_*': 'id_*', # Handled manually for now to match any id field\n                'ebook_access': 'ebook_access',\n                # IA\n                'has_fulltext': 'has_fulltext',\n                'ia': 'ia',\n                'ia_collection': 'ia_collection',\n                'ia_box_id': 'ia_box_id',\n                'public_scan_b': 'public_scan_b',\n            }\n\n            def convert_work_field_to_edition_field(\n                field: str,\n            ) -> str | Callable[[str], str] | None:\n                \"\"\"\n                Convert a SearchField name (eg 'title') to the correct fieldname\n                for use in an edition query.\n\n                If no conversion is possible, return None.\n                \"\"\"\n                if field in WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD:\n                    return WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD[field]\n                elif field.startswith('id_'):\n                    return field\n                elif self.is_search_field(field) or field in self.facet_fields:\n                    return None\n                else:\n                    raise ValueError(f'Unknown field: {field}')\n\n            def convert_work_query_to_edition_query(work_query: str) -> str:\n                \"\"\"\n                Convert a work query to an edition query. Mainly involves removing\n                invalid fields, or renaming fields as necessary.\n                \"\"\"\n                q_tree = luqum_parser(work_query)\n                for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n                    if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField) and node.name != '*':\n                        if node.name.startswith('edition.'):\n                            ed_field = node.name.partition('.')[2]\n                        else:\n                            ed_field = node.name\n\n                        new_name = convert_work_field_to_edition_field(ed_field)\n                        if new_name is None:\n                            try:\n                                luqum_remove_child(node, parents)\n                            except EmptyTreeError:\n                                # Deleted the whole tree! Nothing left\n                                return ''\n                        elif isinstance(new_name, str):\n                            parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n                            # Prefixing with + makes the field mandatory\n                            if isinstance(\n                                parent,\n                                (\n                                    luqum.tree.Not,\n                                    luqum.tree.Prohibit,\n                                    luqum.tree.OrOperation,\n                                ),\n                            ):\n                                node.name = new_name\n                            else:\n                                node.name = f'+{new_name}'\n                            if new_name == 'key':\n                                # need to convert eg 'edition_key:OL123M' to\n                                # 'key:(/books/OL123M)'. Or\n                                # key:(/books/OL123M OR /books/OL456M)\n                                for n, n_parents in luqum_traverse(node.expr):\n                                    if isinstance(\n                                        n, (luqum.tree.Word, luqum.tree.Phrase)\n                                    ):\n                                        val = (\n                                            n.value\n                                            if isinstance(n, luqum.tree.Word)\n                                            else n.value[1:-1]\n                                        )\n                                        val = val.removeprefix('/books/')\n                                        n.value = f'\"/books/{val}\"'\n                        elif callable(new_name):\n                            # Replace this node with a new one\n                            # First process the expr\n                            new_expr = convert_work_query_to_edition_query(\n                                str(node.expr)\n                            )\n                            new_node = luqum.tree.Group(\n                                luqum_parser(new_name(new_expr))\n                            )\n                            if parents:\n                                luqum_replace_child(parents[-1], node, new_node)\n                            else:\n                                return convert_work_query_to_edition_query(\n                                    str(new_node)\n                                )\n                        else:\n                            # Shouldn't happen\n                            raise ValueError(f'Invalid new_name: {new_name}')\n                return str(q_tree)\n\n            # Move over all fq parameters that can be applied to editions.\n            # These are generally used to handle facets.\n            editions_fq = ['type:edition']\n            for param_name, param_value in cur_solr_params:\n                if param_name != 'fq' or param_value.startswith('type:'):\n                    continue\n                field_name, field_val = param_value.split(':', 1)\n                if ed_field := convert_work_field_to_edition_field(field_name):\n                    editions_fq.append(f'{ed_field}:{field_val}')\n            for fq in editions_fq:\n                new_params.append(('editions.fq', fq))\n\n            user_lang = convert_iso_to_marc(self.lang) or 'eng'\n\n            ed_q = convert_work_query_to_edition_query(str(work_q_tree))\n            # Note that if there is no edition query (because no fields in\n            # the user's work query apply), we use the special value *:* to\n            # match everything, but still get boosting.\n            new_params.append(('userEdQuery', ed_q or '*:*'))\n            # Needs to also set this on the editions subquery; subqueries appear\n            # to have their own scope for template parameters, so in order\n            # for `userEdQuery` to be available to `editions.q`, we will\n            # need to specify it twice.\n            new_params.append(('editions.userEdQuery', ed_q or '*:*'))\n            new_params.append(('editions.ol.label', 'EDITION_MATCH'))\n\n            full_ed_query = '({{!edismax bq=\"{bq}\" v={v} qf=\"{qf}\"}})'.format(\n                # See qf in work_query\n                qf='text alternative_title^4 author_name^4 chapter^4',\n                # Reading from the url parameter userEdQuery. This lets us avoid\n                # having to try to escape the query in order to fit inside this\n                # other query.\n                v='$userEdQuery',\n                # bq (boost query): Boost which edition is promoted to the top\n                bq=' '.join(\n                    (\n                        f'language:{user_lang}^40',\n                        'ebook_access:public^10',\n                        'ebook_access:borrowable^8',\n                        'ebook_access:printdisabled^2',\n                        'cover_i:*^2',\n                    )\n                ),\n            )\n\n        if ed_q or len(editions_fq) > 1:\n            # The elements in _this_ edition query should cause works not to\n            # match _at all_ if matching editions are not found\n            new_params.append(\n                ('fullEdQuery', cast(str, full_ed_query) if ed_q else '*:*')\n            )\n            q = (\n                f'+{full_work_query} '\n                # This is using the special parent query syntax to, on top of\n                # the user's `full_work_query`, also only find works which have\n                # editions matching the edition query.\n                # Also include edition-less works (i.e. edition_count:0)\n                '+('\n                '_query_:\"{!parent which=type:work v=$fullEdQuery filters=$editions.fq}\" '\n                'OR edition_count:0'\n                ')'\n            )\n            new_params.append(('q', q))\n        else:\n            new_params.append(('q', full_work_query))\n\n        if highlight:\n            highlight_fields = ('subject', 'chapter')\n            try:\n                # This can throw the EmptyTreeError if nothing remains in the query\n                highlight_query = luqum_deepcopy(work_q_tree)\n                luqum_remove_field(\n                    highlight_query,\n                    lambda f: f not in highlight_fields,\n                )\n                new_params.append(('hl', 'true'))\n                # NOTE: This only applies to the work, really\n                new_params.append(('hl.fl', ','.join(highlight_fields)))\n                new_params.append(('hl.q', str(highlight_query)))\n                new_params.append(('hl.snippets', '10'))\n                # we can't trim e.g. chapter since it has a specific structure with the pipes\n                new_params.append(('hl.fragsize', '0'))\n            except EmptyTreeError:\n                # nothing to highlight\n                pass\n\n        if full_ed_query:\n            edition_fields = {\n                f.split('.', 1)[1] for f in solr_fields if f.startswith('editions.')\n            }\n            if not edition_fields:\n                edition_fields = solr_fields - {\n                    # Default to same fields as for the work...\n                    'editions:[subquery]',\n                    # but exclude the author fields since they're primarily work data;\n                    # they only exist on editions to improve search matches.\n                    'author_name',\n                    'author_key',\n                    'author_alternative_name',\n                    'author_facet',\n                }\n            # The elements in _this_ edition query will match but not affect\n            # whether the work appears in search results\n            new_params.append(\n                (\n                    'editions.q',\n                    # Here we use the special terms parser to only filter the\n                    # editions for a given, already matching work '_root_' node.\n                    f'({{!terms f=_root_ v=$row.key}}) AND {full_ed_query}',\n                )\n            )\n            new_params.append(('editions.rows', '1'))\n            new_params.append(('editions.fl', ','.join(edition_fields)))\n        return new_params\n\n    def add_non_solr_fields(\n        self,\n        non_solr_fields: set[str],\n        solr_result: dict,\n    ) -> None:\n        from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.models import Edition, Work\n\n        prefixed_fields = {\n            prefixed_field\n            for field in non_solr_fields\n            for prefixed_field in (\n                (field,) if '.' in field else (f'work.{field}', f'editions.{field}')\n            )\n        }\n        need_works = any(field.startswith('work.') for field in prefixed_fields)\n        need_editions = any(field.startswith('editions.') for field in prefixed_fields)\n\n        # e.g. lcc:[NC1 TO NC1000] to lcc:[NC-0001.00000000 TO NC-1000.00000000]\n    # for proper range search\n    val = sf.children[0]\n    if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):\n        normed_range = normalize_lcc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)\n        if normed_range:\n            val.low.value, val.high.value = normed_range\n    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word):\n        if '*' in val.value and not val.value.startswith('*'):\n            #   lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 1\n    return escaped_query\n\n\ndef fully_escape_query(query: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to convert a query to basically a plain lucene string.\n\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query(\"foo's bar\")\n    \"foo\\\\\\\\'s bar\"\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> tuple[Word, BaseOperation | None] | None:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n    phrase: bool = False,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'publisher_facet': 'Running Press'}, phrase=True)\n    'publisher_facet:\"Running Press\"'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            (\n                f'{k}:\"{fully_escape_query(v)}\"'\n                if phrase\n                else f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})'\n            )\n            for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n\n\ndef luqum_replace_field(query: Item, replacer: Callable[[str], str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-place replaces portions of a field, as indicated by the replacement function.\n\n    :param query: Passed in the form of a luqum tree\n    :param replacer: function called on each query.\n    \"\"\"\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(query):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField):\n            sf.name = replacer(sf.name)\n\n\ndef luqum_remove_field(query: Item, predicate: Callable[[str], bool]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-place removes fields from a query, as indicated by the predicate function.\n\n    :param query: Passed in the form of a luqum tree\n    :param predicate: function called on each query.\n    \"\"\"\n    for sf, parents in luqum_traverse(query):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and predicate(sf.name):\n            luqum_remove_child(sf, parents)\n\n\ndef luqum_deepcopy(query: Item) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a deep copy of a luqum query tree. Lazy implementation, does a re-parse.\n    \"\"\"\n    return luqum_parser(str(query))\n","numLines":305,"startLine":1,"totalLines":305}}}

But based on my testing, the code looks like it should work...\n\nLet me check the git history to see if there was a recent change that introduced the issue or if the fix is already present:","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1260,"cache_read_input_tokens":51951,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1260},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b2121bf0-cded-4b9e-8ff6-1be92d58ad95","uuid":"331eb100-3239-4063-9acf-506f2d6ca749"}

Iterable[T],\n   125→    key_func: Callable[[T], U],\n   126→    buffer_size: int = 0,\n   127→) -> Generator[list[T], None, None]:\n   128→    current_group: list[T] = []\n   129→    current_key: U | None = None\n   130→    skipped: list[T] = []\n   131→    buffer: list[T] = []\n   132→\n   133→    class CustomIterator:\n   134→        def __init__(self, buffer: list[T], iterable: Iterable[T]):\n   135→            self.buffer = buffer\n   136→            self.iterable = iter(iterable)\n   137→\n   138→        def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[T]:\n   139→            return self\n   140→\n   141→        def __next__(self) -> T:\n   142→            if self.buffer:\n   143→                return self.buffer.pop(0)\n   144→            return next(self.iterable)\n   145→\n   146→    for item in CustomIterator(buffer, iterable):\n   147→        key = key_func(item)\n   148→        if not current_group:\n   149→            # Initial case\n   150→            current_key = key\n   151→            # print('N', key)\n   152→            current_group.append(item)\n   153→        elif key == current_key:\n   154→            # print('+', key)\n   155→            current_group.append(item)\n   156→        else:\n   157→            # print('S', key)\n   158→            skipped.append(item)\n   159→            # Different, add to buffer\n   160→            if len(skipped) > buffer_size:\n   161→                # Run out of buffer, time to move on\n   162→                yield current_group\n   163→                current_group = []\n   164→                current_key = None\n   165→                # Note we don't need to make this recursive because there will always be <buffer length\n   166→                # items in the buffered array\n   167→                buffer.extend(skipped)\n   168→                skipped = []\n   169→\n   170→    if current_group:\n   171→        yield current_group\n   172→    buffer = skipped + buffer\n   173→    if buffer:\n   174→        for key, grp in except KeyboardInterrupt:\n   190→        process.terminate()\n   191→    finally:\n   192→        process.stdout.close()\n   193→        process.stderr.close()\n   194→\n   195→\n   196→def 200→        print(f\"Error parsing log line: {log_line}\\n{e}\")\n   201→        return None\n   202→\n   203→\n   204→def standard_duration_blocks(ms: int) -> str:\n   205→    blocks = (\n   206→        10,\n   207→        100,\n   208→        1000,\n   209→        5000,\n   210→        10000,\n   211→        20000,\n   212→    )\n   213→    for block in blocks:\n   214→        if ms <= block:\n E.g., items=(('a', 1), ('b', 2))\n\n    >>> d = MultiDict()\n    >>> d['x'] = 1\n    >>> d['x'] = 2\n    >>> d['y'] = 3\n    >>> d['x']\n    2\n    >>> d['y']\n    3\n    >>> d['z']\n    Traceback (most recent call last):\n        ...\n    KeyError: 'z'\n    >>> list(d)\n    ['x', 'x', 'y']\n    >>> list(d.items())\n    [('x', 1), ('x', 2), ('y', 3)]\n    >>> list(d.multi_items())\n    [('x', [1, 2]), ('y', [3])]\n    >>> d1 = MultiDict(items=(('a', 1), ('b', 2)), a=('x', 10, 11, 12))\n    [('a', [1, ('x', 10, 11, 12)]), ('b', [2])]\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, items: self._items]\n\n    # Subclasses of MutableMapping should return a dictionary view object for\n    # the values() method, but this implementation returns a list.\n    # element between each set of text content.\n    Optionally truncates contents that exceeds the given max length.\n\n    returns: A reformatted HTML string\n    \"\"\"\n    parser = HTMLTagRemover()\n tuple[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Get a tuple of a location and publisher name from an Internet Archive\n    publisher string. For use in simple location-publisher pairs with one colon.\n\n    >>> get_colon_only_loc_pub('City : Publisher Name')\n    ('City', 'Publisher Name')\n    \"\"\"\n    pairs = pair.split(\":\")\n    if len(pairs) == 2:\n        location = pairs[0].strip(STRIP_CHARS)\n        publisher = pairs[1].strip(STRIP_CHARS)\n\n        return (location, publisher)\n\n    # Fall back to using the entire string as the publisher.\n    return (\"\", pair.strip(STRIP_CHARS))\n\n\ndef get_location_and_publisher(loc_pub: str) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses locations and publisher names out of Internet Archive metadata\n    `publisher` strings. For use when there is no MARC record.\n\n    Returns a tuple of list[location_strings], list[publisher_strings].\n\n    E.g.\n    >>> get_location_and_publisher(\"[New York] : Random House\")\n    (['New York'], ['Random House'])\n    >>> get_location_and_publisher(\"Londres ; New York ; Paris : Berlitz Publishing\")\n    (['Londres', 'New York', 'Paris'], ['Berlitz Publishing'])\n    >>> get_location_and_publisher(\"Paris : Pearson ; San Jose (Calif.) : Adobe\")\n    (['Paris', 'San Jose (Calif.)'], ['Pearson', 'Adobe'])\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if not loc_pub or not isinstance(loc_pub, str):\n        return ([], [])\n\n    if \"Place of publication not identified\" in loc_pub:\n        loc_pub = loc_pub.replace(\"Place of publication not identified\", \"\")\n\n    loc_pub = loc_pub.translate({ord(char): None for char in REPLACE_CHARS})\n\n    # This operates on the notion that anything, even multiple items, to the\n    # left of a colon is a location, and the item immediately to the right of\n    # the colon is a publisher. This can be exploited by using\n    # string.split(\";\") because everything to the 'left' of a colon is a\n    # location, and whatever is to the right is a publisher.\n    if \":\" in loc_pub:\n        locations: list[str] = []\n        publishers: list[str] = []\n        parts = loc_pub.split(\";\") if \";\" in loc_pub else [loc_pub]\n        # Track in indices of values placed into locations or publishers.\n        last_placed_index = 0\n\n        # For each part, look for a semi-colon, then extract everything to\n        # the left as a location, and the item on the right as a publisher.\n        for index, part in enumerate(parts):\n            # This expects one colon per part. Two colons breaks our pattern.\n            # Breaking here gives the chance of extracting a\n            # `location : publisher` from one or more pairs with one semi-colon.\n            if part.count(\":\") > 1:\n                break\n\n            # Per the pattern, anything \"left\" of a colon in a part is a place.\n            if \":\" in part:\n                location, publisher = get_colon_only_loc_pub(part)\n                publishers.append(publisher)\n                # Every index value between last_placed_index and the current\n                # index is a location.\n                for place in parts[last_placed_index:index]:\n                    locations.append(place.strip(STRIP_CHARS))\n                locations.append(location)  # Preserve location order.\n                last_placed_index = index + 1\n\n        # Clean up and empty list items left over from strip() string replacement.\n        locations = [item for item in locations if item]\n        publishers = [item for item in publishers if item]\n\n        return (locations, publishers)\n\n    # Fall back to making the input a list returning that and an empty location.\n    return ([], [loc_pub.strip(STRIP_CHARS)])\n\n\n@public\ndef subject_name_to_key(subject: str, prefix='') -> str:\n    # TODO: DRY with scripts/solr_builder/solr_builder/index_subjects.py\n    if prefix:\n        prefix = prefix.rstrip(':') + ':'\n    return f'/subjects/{prefix}{subject.lower().replace(' ', '_').replace(',', '').replace('/', '')}'\n\n\ndef setup_requests(config=config) -> None:\n    logger.info(\"Setting up requests\")\n\n    logger.info(\"Setting up proxy\")\n    if config.get(\"http_proxy\", \"\"):\n        os.environ['HTTP_PROXY'] = os.environ['http_proxy'] = config.get('http_proxy')\n        os.environ['HTTPS_PROXY'] = os.environ['https_proxy'] = config.get('http_proxy')\n        logger.info('Proxy environment variables are set')\n    else:\n        logger.info(\"No proxy configuration found\")\n\n    logger.info(\"Setting up proxy bypass\")\n    if config.get(\"no_proxy_addresses\", []):\n        no_proxy = \",\".join(config.get(\"no_proxy_addresses\"))\n        os.environ['NO_PROXY'] = os.environ['no_proxy'] = no_proxy\n        logger.info('Proxy bypass environment variables are set')\n    else:\n        logger.info(\"No proxy bypass configuration found\")\n\n    logger.info(\"Requests set up\")\n\n\ndef setup() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Do required initialization\"\"\"\n    # monkey-patch get_markdown to use OL Flavored Markdown\n    view.get_markdown = get_markdown\n\n    # Provide alternate implementations for websafe and commify\n    web.websafe = websafe\n    web.template.Template.FILTERS['.html'] = websafe\n    web.template.Template.FILTERS['.xml'] = websafe\n\n    web.commify = commify\n\n    web.template.Template.globals.update(\n        {\n            'HTML': HTML,\n            'request': Request(),\n            'logger': logging.getLogger(\"openlibrary.template\"),\n            'sum': sum,\n            'websafe': web.websafe,\n        }\n    )\n\n    from openlibrary.core import helpers as h\n\n    web.template.Template.globals.update(h.helpers)\n\n    if config.get('use_gzip') is True:\n        config.middleware.append(GZipMiddleware)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    import doctest\n\n    doctest.testmod()\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1693,"oldLines":7,"newStart":1693,"newLines":7,"lines":["     # A random value will be used if not specified.\n    :param servers: list of  memcached servers, each specified as \"ip:port\"\n    :param timeout: timeout in seconds after which the return value must be updated\n    :param prethread: Function to call on the new thread to set it up\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        f: Callable[P, T],\n        key_prefix: str | None = None,\n        timeout: int = MINUTE_SECS,\n        prethread: Callable[[], None] | None = None,\n        hash_args: bool = False,\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Creates a new memoized function for ``f``.\"\"\"\n        self.f: Callable[P, T] = f\n        self.key_prefix = key_prefix or self._generate_key_prefix()\n        self.timeout = timeout\n\n        self._memcache: memcache.Client | None = None\n\n        self.stats = web.storage(calls=0, hits=0, updates=0, async_updates=0)\n        self.active_threads: dict[str, threading.Thread] = {}\n        self.prethread = prethread\n        self.hash_args = hash_args\n\n    @property\n    def memcache(self) -> memcache.Client:\n        if self._memcache is None:\n            servers = config.get(\"memcache_servers\")\n            if servers:\n                self._memcache = memcache.Client(servers)\n            else:\n                web.debug(\n                    \"Could not find memcache_servers in the configuration. Used dummy memcache.\"\n                )\n                from pymemcache.test.utils import MockMemcacheClient\n\n                self._memcache = MockMemcacheClient()\n\n        return self._memcache\n\n    def _generate_key_prefix(self) -> str:\n        try:\n            prefix = self.f.__name__ + \"_\"\n        except (AttributeError, TypeError):\n            prefix = \"\"\n\n        return prefix + self._random_string(10)\n\n    def _random_string(self, n: int) -> str:\n        chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits\n        return \"\".join(random.choice(chars) for i in range(n))\n\n    def __call__(self, *args: P.args, **kw: P.kwargs) -> T:\n        \"\"\"Memoized function call.\n\n        Returns the cached value when available. Computes and adds the result\n        to memcache when not available. Updates asynchronously after timeout.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        self.stats.calls += 1\n\n        value_time = self.memcache_get(args, kw)\n\n        if value_time is None:\n            self.stats.updates += 1\n            value, t = self.update(*args, **kw)\n        else:\n            self.stats.hits += 1\n\n            value, t = value_time\n            if t + self.timeout < time.time():\n                self.stats.async_updates += 1\n                self.update_async(*args, **kw)\n\n        return value\n\n    def update_async(self, *args: P.args, **kw: P.kwargs) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Starts the update process asynchronously.\"\"\"\n        t = threading.Thread(target=self._update_async_worker, args=args, kwargs=kw)\n        self.active_threads[t.name] = t\n        t.start()\n\n    def _update_async_worker(self, *args: P.args, **kw: P.kwargs) -> None:\n        key = self.compute_key(args, kw) + \"/flag\"\n\n        if not self.memcache.add(key, \"true\"):\n            # already somebody else is computing this value.\n            return\n\n        try:\n            if self.prethread:\n                self.prethread()\n            self.update(*args, **kw)\n        finally:\n            # Remove current thread from active threads\n            self.active_threads.pop(threading.current_thread().name, None)\n\n            # remove the flag\n            self.memcache.delete(key)\n\n    def update(self, *args: P.args, **kw: P.kwargs) -> tuple[T, float]:\n        \"\"\"Computes the value and adds it to memcache.\n\n        Returns the computed value.\n        \"\"\"\n        value = self.f(*args, **kw)\n        t = time.time()\n\n        self.memcache_set(args, kw, value, t)\n        return value, t\n\n    def join_threads(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Waits for all active threads to finish.\n\n        Used only in testing.\n        \"\"\"\n        for name, thread in list(self.active_threads.items()):\n            thread.join()\n\n    def encode_args(self, args: tuple, kw: dict | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Encodes arguments to construct the memcache key.\"\"\"\n        kw = kw or {}\n\n        # strip [ and ] from key\n        a = self.json_encode(list(args))[1:-1]\n\n        if kw:\n            a = a + \"-\" + self.json_encode(kw)\n        if self.hash_args:\n            return f\"{hashlib.md5(a.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()}\"\n        return a\n\n    def compute_key(self, args: tuple, kw: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Computes memcache key for storing result of function call with given arguments.\"\"\"\n        key = self.key_prefix + \"$\" + self.encode_args(args, kw)\n        return key.replace(\n            \" \", \"_\"\n        )  # XXX: temporary fix to handle spaces in the arguments\n\n    def json_encode(self, value: Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"json.dumps without extra spaces.\n\n        memcache doesn't like spaces in the key.\n        \"\"\"\n        return json.dumps(\n            [] if isinstance(value, Nothing) else value,\n            separators=(\",\", \":\"),\n            cls=NothingEncoder,\n        )\n\n    def memcache_set(self, args: tuple, kw: dict, value: T, time: float) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Adds value and time to memcache. Key is computed from the arguments.\"\"\"\n        key = self.compute_key(args, kw)\n        json_data = self.json_encode([value, time])\n\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.set\", key=key)\n        self.memcache.set(key, json_data)\n        stats.end()\n\n    def memcache_delete(self, args: tuple, kw: dict) -> None:\n        key = self.compute_key(args, kw)\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.delete\", key=key)\n        self.memcache.delete(key)\n        stats.end()\n\n    def memcache_delete_by_args(self, *args, **kw):\n        \"\"\"A helper method to let you pass in arguments normally instead of as a tuple and dict\"\"\"\n        self.memcache_delete(args, kw)\n\n    def memcache_get(self, args: tuple, kw: dict) -> tuple[T, float] | None:\n        \"\"\"Reads the value from memcache. Key is computed from the arguments.\n\n        Returns (value, time) when the value is available, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        key = self.compute_key(args, kw)\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.get\", key=key)\n        json_str = cast(str, self.memcache.get(key))\n        stats.end(hit=bool(json_str))\n\n        if json_str:\n            # 5. We must `cast` here. JSON serialization erases types,\n            # so we are telling the type checker to trust us that the\n            # deserialized object matches our generic type T.\n            return cast(tuple[T, float], json.loads(json_str))\n        else:\n            return None\n\n\n####\n\n\nclass Cache:\n    \"\"\"Cache interface.\"\"\"\n\n    def get(self, key):\n        \"\"\"Returns the value for given key. Returns None if that key is not present in the cache.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def set(self, key, value, expires=0):\n        \"\"\"Sets a value in the cache.\n        If expires is non-zero, the cache may delete that entry from the cache after expiry.\n        The implementation can choose to ignore the expires argument.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def add(self, key, value, expires=0):\n        \"\"\"Adds a new entry in the cache. Nothing is done if there is already an entry with the same key.\n\n        Returns True if a new entry is added to the cache.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def delete(self, key):\n        \"\"\"Deletes an entry from the cache. No error is raised if there is no entry in present in the cache with that key.\n\n        Returns True if the key is deleted.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n\nclass MemoryCache(Cache):\n    \"\"\"Cache implementation in memory.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.d = {}\n\n    def get(self, key):\n        return self.d.get(key)\n\n    def set(self, key, value, expires=0):\n        self.d[key] = value\n\n    def add(self, key, value, expires=0):\n        return self.d.setdefault(key, value) is value\n\n    def delete(self, key):\n        return self.d.pop(key, None) is not None\n\n    def clear(self):\n        self.d.clear()\n\n\nclass MemcacheCache(Cache):\n    \"\"\"Cache implementation using memcache.\n\n    The values are json-encoded before adding to memcache and json-decoded on get.\n\n    Expects that the memcache servers are specified in web.config.memcache_servers.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @functools.cached_property\n    def memcache(self):\n        if servers := config.get(\"memcache_servers\", None):\n            return olmemcache.Client(servers)\n        else:\n            web.debug(\n                \"Could not find memcache_servers in the configuration. Used dummy memcache.\"\n            )\n            from pymemcache.test.utils import MockMemcacheClient\n\n            return MockMemcacheClient()\n\n    def _encode_key(self, key: str) -> str:\n        return cast(str, web.safestr(key))\n\n    def get(self, key: str) -> Any:\n        key = self._encode_key(key)\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.get\", key=key)\n        value = self.memcache.get(key)\n        stats.end(hit=value is not None)\n        return value and json.loads(value)\n\n    def get_multi(self, keys: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        keys = [self._encode_key(k) for k in keys]\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.get_multi\")\n        d = self.memcache.get_multi(keys)\n        stats.end(hit=bool(d))\n        return {k: json.loads(v) for k, v in d.items()}\n\n    def set_multi(self, mapping: dict[str, Any], expires=0):\n        mapping = {self._encode_key(k): json.dumps(v) for k, v in mapping.items()}\n        stats.begin(\"memcache.set_multi\")\n        d = self.memcache.set_multi(mapping, expires)\n        stats.end()\n        return d\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: Any, expires=0):\n        key =    value = self.memcache.delete(key)\n        stats.end()\n        return value\n\n\nclass RequestCache(Cache):\n    \"\"\"Request-Local cache.\n\n    The values are cached only in the context of the current request.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def d(self):\n        return web.ctx.setdefault(\"request-local-cache\", {})\n\n    def get(self, key):\n        return self.d.get(key)\n\n    def memcache_cache\n\n\ndef _get_cache(engine):\n    d = {\n        \"memory\": memory_cache,\n        \"memcache\": memcache_cache,\n        \"memcache+memory\": memcache_cache,\n        \"request\": request_cache,\n    }\n    return d.get(engine)\n\n\nclass memoize:\n    \"\"\"Memoize decorator to cache results in various cache engines.\n\n    Usage::\n\n        @cache.memoize(engine=\"memcache\")\n        def some_func(args):\n            pass\n\n    Arguments::\n\n    * engine:\n        Engine to store the results. Available options are:\n            * memory: stores the result in memory.\n            * memcache: stores the result in memcached.\n            * request: stores the result only in the context of the current request.\n\n    * key:\n        key to be used in the cache. If this is a string, arguments are append\n        to it before making the cache-key. If this is a function, it's\n        return-value is used as cache-key and this function is called with the\n        arguments. If not specified, the default value is computed using the\n        function name and module name.\n\n    * expires:\n        The amount of time in seconds the value should be cached. Pass expires=0 to cache indefinitely.\n        (Not yet implemented)\n\n    * background:\n        Indicates that the value must be recomputed in the background after\n        the timeout. Until the new value is ready, the function continue to\n        return the same old value.\n        (not yet implemented)\n\n    * cacheable:\n        Function to determine if the returned value is cacheable. Sometimes it\n        is desirable to not cache return values generated due to error\n        conditions. The cacheable function is called with (key, value) as\n        arguments.\n\n    Advanced Usage:\n\n    Sometimes, it is desirable to store results of related functions in the\n    same cache entry to reduce memory usage. It can be achieved by making the\n    ``key`` function return a tuple of two values. (Not Implemented yet)\n\n        @cache.memoize(engine=\"memcache\", key=lambda page: (page.key, \"history\"))\n        def get_history(page):\n            pass\n\n        @cache.memoize(engine=\"memory\", key=lambda key: (key, \"doc\"))\n        def get_page(key):\n            pass\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        engine: Literal[\"memory\", \"memcache\", \"request\"],\n        key: str | Callable[..., str | tuple],\n        expires: int = 0,\n        background: bool = False,\n        cacheable: Callable | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.cache = _get_cache(engine)\n        self.keyfunc = (\n            key if callable(key) else functools.partial(build_memcache_key, key)\n        )\n        self.cacheable = cacheable\n        self.expires = expires\n\n    def __call__(self, f):\n        \"\"\"Returns the memoized version of f.\"\"\"\n\n        if inspect.iscoroutinefunction(f):\n            # Async function - return async wrapper\n            @functools.wraps(f)\n            async def async_func(*args, **kwargs):\n                \"\"\"The memoized async function.\n\n                If this is the first call with these arguments, function :attr:`f` is called and the return value is cached.\n                Otherwise, value from the cache is returned.\n                \"\"\"\n                key = self.keyfunc(*args, **kwargs)\n                value = self.cache_get(key)\n                if value is None:\n                    value = await f(*args, **kwargs)\n                    self.cache_set(key, value)\n                return value\n\n            return async_func\n        else:\n            # Sync function - return sync wrapper (existing behavior)\n            @functools.wraps(f)\n            def func(*args, **kwargs):\n                \"\"\"The memoized function.\n\n                If this is the first call with these arguments, function :attr:`f` is called and the return value is cached.\n                Otherwise, value from the cache is returned.\n                \"\"\"\n                key = self.keyfunc(*args, **kwargs)\n                value = self.cache_get(key)\n                if value is None:\n                    value = f(*args, **kwargs)\n                    self.cache_set(key, value)\n                return value\n\n            return func\n\n    def cache_get(self, key: str | tuple):\n        \"\"\"Reads value of a key from the cache.\n\n        When key is a string, this is equvivalant to::\n\n            return cache[key]\n\n        When key is a 2-tuple, this is equvivalant to::\n\n            k0, k1 = key\n            return cache[k0][k1]\n        \"\"\"\n        if isinstance(key, tuple):\n            k0, k1 = key\n            d = self.cache.get(k0)\n            return d and d.get(k1)\n        else:\n            return self.cache.get(key)\n\n    def cache_set(self, key: str | tuple, value):\n        \"\"\"Sets a key to a given value in the cache.\n\n        When key is a string, this is equvivalant to::\n\n            cache[key] = value\n\n        When key is a 2-tuple, this is equvivalant to::\n\n            k0, k1 = key\n            cache[k0][k1] = value\n        \"\"\"\n        # When cacheable is provided, use it to determine whether or not the cache should be updated.\n        if self.cacheable and self.cacheable(key, value) is False:\n            return\n\n        if isinstance(key, tuple):\n            k1, k2 = key\n            d = self.cache.get(k1) or {}\n            d[k2] = value\n            return self.cache.set(k1, d, expires=self.expires)\n        else:\n            return self.cache.set(key, value, expires=self.expires)\n\n\ndef build_memcache_key(prefix: str, *args, **kw) -> str:\n    key = prefix\n\n    if args:\n        key += \"-\" + json.dumps(args, separators=(\",\", \":\"), sort_keys=True)[1:-1]\n    if kw:\n        key += \"-\" + json.dumps(kw, separators=(\",\", \":\"), sort_keys=True)\n\n    return key\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":37,"oldLines":7,"newStart":37,"newLines":7,"lines":[" T = TypeVar('T')"," "," ","-class memcache_memoize[**P, T]:","+class memcache_memoize:","     \"\"\"Memoizes a function, caching its return values in memcached for each input."," ","     After the timeout, a new thread is spawned to update the value and the old"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

On search results which don't have an edition selected, we want to display\n    # IA copies first.\n    # Issue is that an edition can be provided by multiple providers; we can easily\n    # choose the correct copy when on an edition, but on a solr work record, with all\n    # copies of all editions aggregated, it's more difficult.\n    # So we do some ugly ocaid sniffing to try to guess :/ Idea being that we ignore\n    # OCAIDs that look like they're from other providers.\n    has_edition = isinstance(ed_or_solr, Edition) or ed_or_solr['key'].startswith(\n        '/books/'\n    )\n    prefer_ia = not has_edition\n    if prefer_ia:\n        ia_ocaids = [\n            ocaid\n            #
