Map provider name to position/ranking\n    provider_rank_lookup: dict[AbstractBookProvider | None, int] = {\n        provider: i for i, provider in enumerate(provider_order)\n    }\n\n    # Here, we prefer the ia editions\n    augmented_editions = [(edition, get_book_provider(edition)) for edition in editions]\n\n    best = multisort_best(\n        augmented_editions,\n        [\n            # Prefer the providers closest to the top of the list\n            ('min', lambda rec: provider_rank_lookup.get(rec[1], float('inf'))),\n            # Prefer the editions with the most fields\n            ('max', lambda rec: len(dict(rec[0]))),\n            #  :param str s: A book title to normalize and strip.\n    :return: a lowercase string with no spaces, containing the main words of the title.\n    \"\"\"\n    if m := re_brackets.match(s):\n        s = purposes.\n    TODO: Expand using\n    https://web.archive.org/web/20230320141510/https://www.loc.gov/marc/bibliographic/bdapndxf.html\n    or something sensible.\n    \"\"\"\n    if s.lower().startswith('the '):\n        s = s[4:]\n    elif s.lower().startswith('a '):\n        s = s[2:]\n    return s\n\n\ndef add_db_name(rec: dict) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    db_name = Author name followed by dates.\n    adds 'db_name' in place for each author.\n    \"\"\"\n    if 'authors' not in rec:\n        return\n\n    for a in rec['authors'] or []:\n        date = None\n        if 'date' in a:\n            assert 'birth_date' not in a\n            assert 'death_date' not in a\n            date = a['date']\n        elif 'birth_date' in a or 'death_date' in a:\n            date = \"\"\"\n    Returns an expanded representation of an edition dict,\n    usable for accurate comparisons between existing and new\n    records.\n\n    :param dict rec: Import edition representation\n    :return: An expanded version of an edition dict\n        more titles, normalized + short\n        all isbns in \"isbn\": []\n        authors have db_name (name with dates)\n    \"\"\"\n '|||',\n    ):\n        expanded_rec['publish_country'] = rec['publish_country']\n    for f in (\n        'lccn',\n        'publishers',\n        'publish_date',\n        'number_of_pages',\n        'authors',\n        'contribs',\n    ):\n        if f in rec:\n            expanded_rec[f] = rec[f]\n    add_db_name(expanded_rec)\n    return expanded_rec\n\n\ndef build_titles(title: str) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Uses a full title to create normalized and short title versions.\n    Used for expanding a set of title variants for matching,\n    not for storing on records or display.\n\n    :param str title: Full title of an edition\n    :rtype: dict\n    :return: An expanded set of title variations\n    \"\"\"\n    normalized_title = normalize(title)\n    titles = [  # TODO: how different and helpful are these titles variants?\n        title,\n        normalized_title,\n        strip_articles(normalized_title),\n    ]\n    if m := re_amazon_title_paren.match(normalized_title):\n        titles.append(m.group(1))\n        titles.append(strip_articles(m.group(1)))\n\n    return {\n        'full_title': title,\n        'normalized_title': normalized_title,\n        'titles': ThresholdResult:\n    field = 'publish_country'\n    if field not in e1 or field not in e2:\n        return (field, 'value missing', 0)\n    if e1[field] == e2[field]:\n        return (field, 'match', 40)\n    # West Berlin (wb) == Germany (gw)\n    if e1[field] in ('gw ', 'wb ') and e2[field] in ('gw ', 'wb '):\n        return (field, 'match', 40)\n    return (field, 'mismatch', -205)\n\n\ndef compare_lccn(e1: dict, e2: dict) -> ThresholdResult:\n    field = 'lccn'\n    if field not in e1 or field not in e2:\n        return (field, 'value missing', 0)\n    if e1[field] == e2[field]:\n        return (field, 'match', 200)\n    return (field, 'mismatch', -320)\n\n\ndef compare_date(e1: dict, e2: dict) -> ThresholdResult:\n    if ('publish_date' not in e1) and ('publish_date' not in e2):\n        return ('date', 'values missing', 0)\n    if ('publish_date' in e1) ^ ('publish_date' in e2):\n        return ('date', 'value missing', DATE_MISMATCH)\n    if e1['publish_date'] == e2['publish_date']:\n        return ('date', 'exact match', 200)\n    try:\n        e1_pub = int(e1['publish_date'])\n        e2_pub = int(e2['publish_date'])\n        if within(e1_pub, e2_pub, 2):\n            return ('date', '+/-2 years', -25)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        pass\n    return ('date', 'mismatch', DATE_MISMATCH)\n\n\ndef compare_isbn(e1: dict, e2: dict) -> ThresholdResult:\n    if len(e1['isbn']) == 0 or len(e2['isbn']) == 0:\n        return ('ISBN', 'missing', 0)\n    for i in e1['isbn']:\n        for j in e2['isbn']:\n            if i == j:\n                return ('ISBN', 'match', ISBN_MATCH)\n    return ('ISBN', 'mismatch', -225)\n\n\ndef level1_match(e1: dict, e2: dict) -> list[ThresholdResult]:\n    \"\"\"\n    :param dict e1: Expanded Edition, score.append(compare_publisher(e1, e2))\n    score.append(compare_authors(e1, e2))\n    return score\n\n\ndef compare_author_fields(e1_authors, e2_authors) -> bool:\n    for i in e1_authors:\n normalize(j['name']).strip('.'):\n                return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef compare_author_keywords(e1_authors, e2_authors) -> ThresholdResult:\n    max_score = 0\n    for i in On search results which don't have an edition selected, we want to display\n    # IA copies first.\n    # Issue is that an edition can be provided by multiple providers; we can easily\n    # choose the correct copy when on an edition, but on a solr work record, with all\n    # copies of all editions aggregated, it's more difficult.\n    # So we do some ugly ocaid sniffing to try to guess :/ Idea being that we ignore\n    # OCAIDs that look like they're from other providers.\n    has_edition = isinstance(ed_or_solr, Edition) or ed_or_solr['key'].startswith(\n        '/books/'\n    )\n    prefer_ia = not has_edition\n    if prefer_ia:\n        ia_ocaids = [\n            ocaid\n            # Map provider name to position/ranking\n    provider_rank_lookup: dict[AbstractBookProvider | None, int] = {\n        provider: i for i, provider in enumerate(provider_order)\n    }\n\n    # Here, we prefer the ia editions\n    augmented_editions = [(edition, get_book_provider(edition)) for edition in editions]\n\n    best = multisort_best(\n        augmented_editions,\n        [\n            # Prefer the providers closest to the top of the list\n            ('min', lambda rec: provider_rank_lookup.get(rec[1], float('inf'))),\n            # Prefer the editions with the most fields\n            ('max', lambda rec: len(dict(rec[0]))),\n            # TODO: Language would go in this queue somewhere\n        ],\n    )\n\n    return best or (None, None)\n\n\ndef get_solr_keys() -> list[str]:\n    return [p.solr_key for p in PROVIDER_ORDER if p.solr_key]\n\n\nsetattr(get_book_provider, 'ia', get_book_provider_by_name('ia'))  #
